facade_colour: rich red-brown brick with polychromatic buff brick bands and yellow-green painted trim

hcd_data.architectural_style: House-form, Semi-detached, Bay-and-Gable (confirmed – HCD Plan Vol. 2)
hcd_data.statement_of_contribution: Each property has value as a nineteenth-century House-form building that reflects vernacular interpretations of Victorian architectural styles. Through its construction date and typology, each property is directly associated with the District’s early residential development from the 1850s to the early 1900s. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they retain many of their original building features including a cross gabled roofline, double-height bay windows, fenestration pattern, and gables. 24 Bellevue Avenue also retains its brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and wooden bargeboards and brackets. Contextually, each property shares a consistent setback, scale, and massing with the other contributing properties on the street. Each property supports and maintains the residential character of the streetscape and is functionally, visually, and historically linked to the District.

context.street_character: One-way | Residential street | Tree-lined | Heritage character

facade_detail.composition: The street elevation spans roughly 5.2 metres across 3 storeys and is capped by a cross-gable, consistent with its semi-detached bay-and-gable house-form typology.
facade_detail.opening_rhythm: The facade keeps a domestic opening rhythm of 2, 2, 1 window bays from lower to upper levels, using double-hung sash windows, and 1 principal entrance.
facade_detail.heritage_expression: Character-defining facade elements include decorative brick, original windows, bay window.
facade_detail.heritage_summary: 24 Bellevue Avenue also retains its brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and wooden bargeboards and brackets. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they retain many of their original building features including a cross gabled roofline, double-height bay windows, fenestration pattern, and gables.

facade_description: Three-storey semi-detached bay-and-gable in rich red-brown brick with striking polychromatic buff brick banding courses. A double-height canted bay dominates the facade, with segmental arched brick voussoirs over all openings. Yellow-green painted trim on bargeboard, brackets, window frames, and door is freshly maintained and visually distinctive. One of the best-preserved Victorian facades on Bellevue Ave, with a covered front porch and original 2-over-2 glazing pattern largely intact.
heritage_summary: 24 Bellevue Avenue also retains its brick cladding with polychromatic brickwork, and wooden bargeboards and brackets. Constructed as a semi-detached pair, they retain many of their original building features including a cross gabled roofline, double-height bay windows, fenestration pattern, and gables.

photo_observations.facade_colour_observed: rich red-brown brick with polychromatic buff/cream brick banding and yellow-green painted wood trim throughout
photo_observations.facade_condition_notes: Excellent condition. One of the best-preserved Victorian bay-and-gable facades on Bellevue Ave. Polychromatic brickwork intact. Yellow-green painted trim on all window frames, bargeboard, door, and porch elements appears freshly maintained.
photo_observations.window_details: Ground floor: 2 windows in projecting bay with yellow-green painted frames, segmental arched brick heads. Second floor: 2 taller windows in bay, yellow-green frames. Gable: 1 smaller window. Star decoration hanging in one ground-floor window. All windows appear to retain original proportions and 2-over-2 glazing pattern.
photo_observations.door_details: Yellow-green painted panelled door with glazed upper panel. Segmental arched brick head above door with decorative voussoir. Transom light above.
photo_observations.cornice_details: Decorative wooden brackets at eave line, painted yellow-green to match trim
photo_observations.bay_window_details: Canted three-sided bay spanning ground and second floor. Brick construction with segmental arched window heads. Bay is the primary facade feature, consistent with bay-and-gable typology.
photo_observations.porch_details: Covered front porch with wooden posts and railing, extends across portion of facade. Steps up from grade. Yellow-green painted elements.
photo_observations.decorative_details: Outstanding polychromatic brickwork: buff/cream brick banding courses contrasting with red-brown field brick. Segmental arched brick voussoirs over all openings. Decorative wooden bargeboard at gable peak with elaborate scrollwork, painted yellow-green. Wooden brackets at eaves. The polychromatic brickwork and yellow-green trim make this one of the most visually distinctive facades on Bellevue Ave.
photo_observations.roof_type_observed: Cross-gable with steep pitch, dark shingles
photo_observations.alterations_visible: Minimal. Building appears remarkably well-preserved. Trim colour (yellow-green) may not be original but is sympathetic to the era. A balcony/deck visible at upper rear. Overall heritage character is excellent.
photo_observations.notes: Outstanding example of a Victorian bay-and-gable semi-detached. Rich red-brown brick with polychromatic buff banding. Yellow-green painted trim on all woodwork including bargeboard, brackets, window frames, and door. Double-height projecting bay. Covered front porch. Star decoration in window. HCD notes this property specifically retains polychromatic brickwork and wooden bargeboards and brackets. One of the best-preserved on the street.
